questions.question: According to the episode, what kind of righteousness does the Reformed view emphasize in justification?
options: Christ's righteousness imputed on the basis of union with Christ by faith
Inherent justice produced entirely without sacramental grace
Righteousness earned solely by post-baptismal works without grace
A righteousness that is identical to moral perfection in this life
questions.answer: Christ's righteousness imputed on the basis of union with Christ by faith

questions.question: The episode says Roman Catholic initial justification (as defined from Trent) is accomplished by which means?
options: Ordination alone
Baptism
Confirmation alone
Private confession before baptism
questions.answer: Baptism

questions.question: In the episode's summary, what does Trent teach about an unregenerate person's ability to truly merit eternal life?
options: Merit applies only after final justification, never initially
Merit is impossible for anyone, regenerate or not
An unregenerate man cannot truly merit eternal life or rewards
An unregenerate man can merit condignly by natural virtue alone
questions.answer: An unregenerate man cannot truly merit eternal life or rewards

questions.question: The speaker cites Oliver Rafferty to make which point about Trent?
options: Trent explicitly rejected all Thomist opinions
Trent avoided discussing merit entirely
Trent presented only one monolithic view of justification with no debates
Commentators warn it is a mistake to think Trent presented only a single view of justification
questions.answer: Commentators warn it is a mistake to think Trent presented only a single view of justification

questions.question: Which medieval schools are named as having different understandings of true merit?
options: Thomists, Scotists, and nominalists
Franciscans, Jesuits, and Dominicans only
Cappadocians, Nestorians, and Monophysites
Scholastics, mystics, and canon lawyers exclusively
questions.answer: Thomists, Scotists, and nominalists

questions.question: What outcome does the episode claim followed the Augustinian emphasis on imputation at Trent?
options: It became the official definition without change
Views of imputation and the denial that works can be truly meritorious were anathematized
It was adopted verbatim into the Roman Catechism without debate
It was deferred to a later Vatican council
questions.answer: Views of imputation and the denial that works can be truly meritorious were anathematized

questions.question: The episode claims the exact meaning of which Trent-related idea was left undefined, creating ambiguity?
options: The canon of Scripture
The date of Easter
True merit
The number of sacraments
questions.answer: True merit

questions.question: How does the episode characterize the Vulgate Latin trajectory for the verb 'justify'?
options: It was rejected by Jerome and Augustine
It reflects a transformative sense tied to 'make just,' unlike the more judicial Greek
It perfectly preserves Paul's purely ceremonial meaning
It eliminates any forensic courtroom imagery entirely from the New Testament
questions.answer: It reflects a transformative sense tied to 'make just,' unlike the more judicial Greek

questions.question: According to the episode, Ludwig Ott paraphrases Trent's justification as including which negative description?
options: A true eradication of sin
A mere external legal fiction with no inner change
Only a future eschatological judgment with no present grace
A temporary forgiveness unrelated to adoption
questions.answer: A true eradication of sin

questions.question: The episode ties Trent's rejection of concupiscence as inherently sinful to which consequence?
options: It makes it harder to claim baptism regenerates infants
It helps justify claiming sin is truly eradicated so works can be truly meritorious
It eliminates the need for confession after mortal sin
It removes the distinction between faith and hope
questions.answer: It helps justify claiming sin is truly eradicated so works can be truly meritorious

questions.question: Which sacraments are mentioned as initiating, restoring after mortal sin, and sustaining perseverance?
options: Confirmation; baptism; matrimony
Penance only; no other sacraments are mentioned
Baptism; reconciliation (confession and penance); Eucharist
Matrimony; holy orders; anointing of the sick
questions.answer: Baptism; reconciliation (confession and penance); Eucharist

questions.question: Richard Muller is cited for which Protestant definition of justification?
options: Adoption alone without forgiveness or imputation
A counting or reckoning righteous—God forgives and counts sinners righteous on the basis of faith in Christ
A purely metaphorical story with no legal meaning
Moral transformation identical to sanctification without distinction
questions.answer: A counting or reckoning righteous—God forgives and counts sinners righteous on the basis of faith in Christ

questions.question: The Westminster Confession is quoted to say saving faith is the alone instrument of justification, yet also that it is not what?
options: Not alone in the person justified—accompanied by other saving graces; not dead but works through love
Not ever accompanied by hope
Not related to trust in Christ
Not necessary if one has charity
questions.answer: Not alone in the person justified—accompanied by other saving graces; not dead but works through love

questions.question: The episode maps Reformed subdivisions of saving faith to which three Latin labels?
options: Fides, spes, caritas only
Justitia, gratia, meritum
Lex, gospel, tradition
Notitia, assensus, fiducia
questions.answer: Notitia, assensus, fiducia

questions.question: Why does the episode say James's discussion of demons 'believe and tremble' does not contradict Pauline justification?
options: Because demons are secretly justified in Catholic teaching
Because Paul never uses the word faith
Because James refers to mere belief/assent, not loving trust in Christ's redemption
Because James denies justification entirely
questions.answer: Because James refers to mere belief/assent, not loving trust in Christ's redemption

questions.question: Domingo de Soto is presented as defining faith's substance primarily as what?
options: Hope and love without assent
Intellectual persuasion and assent to revealed truth
Vision of God's essence in this life
Ecstatic prophecy without doctrine
questions.answer: Intellectual persuasion and assent to revealed truth

questions.question: What is 'fides caritate formata' in the episode's Catholic section?
options: Faith formed by charity—distinguished yet related to Protestant 'living faith' language
Faith without any need for charity
Charity without faith
A purely political oath in Tridentine canon law
questions.answer: Faith formed by charity—distinguished yet related to Protestant 'living faith' language

questions.question: The episode locates the deepest disagreement less in the words 'faith alone' and more in which contrast?
options: Whether Jesus is divine
Whether the Old Testament is Scripture
Whether councils can ever meet
Whether what merits eternal life is extrinsic (outside us) or intrinsic (inside us)
questions.answer: Whether what merits eternal life is extrinsic (outside us) or intrinsic (inside us)

questions.question: Which Pauline autobiographical passage does the episode use to argue even the apostle is not already perfect?
options: 1 Timothy 3 only
Revelation 21 only
Philippians 3 (alongside Romans 7 themes in the surrounding argument)
Acts 17 only
questions.answer: Philippians 3 (alongside Romans 7 themes in the surrounding argument)

questions.question: How does the episode use James's statement about keeping the whole law yet offending in one point?
options: To teach that ceremonial law alone saves
To argue the mortal/venial distinction fails against the law's whole-demand seriousness
To prove venial sins do not matter
To deny that James is canonical
questions.answer: To argue the mortal/venial distinction fails against the law's whole-demand seriousness

questions.question: The episode contrasts Trent's claim that the just man can fulfill the law with which three witnesses it prefers?
options: Scripture, good reason, and experience
Only church tradition
Only papal encyclicals
Only canon law
questions.answer: Scripture, good reason, and experience

questions.question: Hubert Jedin is cited in connection with which pastoral question at Trent?
options: How to calculate indulgence tariffs
Whether Latin should replace Greek in seminaries
How to organize monastic libraries
On what can a man rely when he thinks of God's judgment in order not to despair
questions.answer: On what can a man rely when he thinks of God's judgment in order not to despair

questions.question: The episode cites Ephesians to emphasize which theme about believers?
options: Temple sacrifices as ongoing propitiation
Apostolic succession lists
Union with Christ—raised and seated in heavenly places; saved by grace
Circumcision as the instrument of justification
questions.answer: Union with Christ—raised and seated in heavenly places; saved by grace

questions.question: What series milestone does the episode announce at the end?
options: It announces a break with no further episodes planned
It is the last episode of the six-part 'Protesting or Catholic' series
It begins a new twelve-part series
It is a mid-series recap only
questions.answer: It is the last episode of the six-part 'Protesting or Catholic' series
